div.h2: Three-Level View of a recv() Call

div.p: Thread state machine RUNNING → syscall → BLOCKED (if recv buffer empty) BLOCKED → data arrives → RUNNABLE → scheduler picks up → RUNNING → recv() returns
Return value ≤ kernel recv buffer occupancy at that instant A single write() on sender → may arrive as 2, 3, or 20 recv() calls on receiver. Correct code always loops.

text.tspan: Copy: kernel recv buf → user buf

div.p: CPU is NOT involved in DMA transfer. Frame data moves directly: Wire → NIC buffer → kernel ring buffer via bus-master DMA. CPU only wakes on the interrupt.
